content: The Next Web https://thenextweb.com Original and proudly opinionated perspectives for Generation T Fri, 10 Jul 2026 07:57:19 +0000 en-US hourly 1 TNW-API Special delivery: Italy’s postal service joins the AI infrastructure race https://thenextweb.com/news/poste-italiane-ai-infrastructure-race Fri, 10 Jul 2026 07:54:47 +0000 TheNextWeb=927388b963f796f8c6f170841d77b930 Italy’s postal service has spent a century and a half moving letters, parcels and pension payments around the country. Now it wants to move data. Poste Italiane, which still hands out state pensions through roughly 12,600 branches, has cast itself as an unlikely contender in Europe’s scramble to build the infrastructure behind AI. The bet […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> OpenAI is killing its ChatGPT Atlas browser less than a year after launch https://thenextweb.com/news/openai-chatgpt-atlas-browser-shutdown-superapp Fri, 10 Jul 2026 07:36:13 +0000 TheNextWeb=429d21670093a08ffb061528b0561719 OpenAI is shutting down ChatGPT Atlas, its AI-powered web browser, less than a year after launching it. The company is targeting 9 August for deprecation, The Verge reports. Atlas only arrived in October 2025, pitched as a browser that could carry out tasks on a user’s behalf. Nine months later, it is being retired. The move […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> Google will now tell you which ads were made with AI, if the advertiser admits it https://thenextweb.com/news/google-discloses-ai-made-ads-my-ad-center Thu, 09 Jul 2026 19:50:05 +0000 TheNextWeb=9860cc7cd16f00e18fe2bb91751cf172 Google is rolling out a feature that flags when an advertisement was made using AI. The label will indicate if an ad was created or edited with generative tools, TechCrunch reports. The disclosure appears in the “My Ad Center” panel, reachable via the three-dot menu or info icon on ads. It covers ads across Google Search, […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> Bezos opens Blue Origin to outside investors for the first time in 26 years https://thenextweb.com/news/bezos-blue-origin-opens-outside-investors-10bn Thu, 09 Jul 2026 19:39:23 +0000 TheNextWeb=33ab9624730d4c8d3768e13133f2bf5e Jeff Bezos is letting outside investors into Blue Origin for the first time since he founded it in 2000. The rocket company is seeking about $10bn in fresh capital at a $130bn pre-money valuation, according to CNBC. For 26 years, Bezos bankrolled the company himself, selling billions in Amazon stock rather than sharing ownership. That solo-funding […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> News outlets ask a judge to sanction OpenAI in their landmark copyright fight https://thenextweb.com/news/news-outlets-openai-sanctions-copyright-fight Thu, 09 Jul 2026 19:20:28 +0000 TheNextWeb=f17bb62cdc4036ac67723bc7be51c3fa A group of news publishers has asked a federal judge to impose sanctions on OpenAI. The New York Times, the Daily News, and others allege the ChatGPT maker is concealing evidence central to their copyright case, the Associated Press reports. A filing on Thursday in Manhattan federal court claims OpenAI “chose obstruction” over handing over datasets […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> New Jersey could ban Tesla’s Robotaxi with one line about sensors https://thenextweb.com/news/new-jersey-robotaxi-bill-lidar-tesla-ban Thu, 09 Jul 2026 19:15:34 +0000 TheNextWeb=41b11f26683f7bfefe9ef310bccc37d5 For more than a decade, the biggest fight in self-driving cars has played out in boardrooms and engineering labs. New Jersey now wants to settle it in law. A bill moving through the state legislature would force any company running fully driverless cars in New Jersey to fit them with a camera system plus two […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> Musk says he always wanted SpaceX staff to get rich, and thousands just did https://thenextweb.com/news/spacex-ipo-employee-millionaires-equity Thu, 09 Jul 2026 19:06:28 +0000 TheNextWeb=9b413b4bd0d06d3f8e786d7f172e20e8 Elon Musk says SpaceX’s blockbuster listing has probably made thousands of his employees millionaires. Asked on The Sean Hannity Show about a former welder whose stock topped $1m, he said the story was far from unique. “It’s not just one welder, it’s several thousand people who were working on the production line,” Musk said, adding […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> AI’s land rush is reaching Native American reservations https://thenextweb.com/news/ais-land-rush-is-reaching-native-american-reservations Thu, 09 Jul 2026 18:55:29 +0000 TheNextWeb=264d0d886567838568c8a14ece683759 The scramble to build AI data centres is pushing developers toward Native American land. Indigenous-led group Honor the Earth says it is tracking more than 100 proposed projects on or near tribal and rural territory. The appeal for developers is practical. Large land-based tribes often have space, water rights, and power access, and reservations can offer tax […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> Zuckerberg confirms Meta is eyeing an AI cloud business to rent out its compute https://thenextweb.com/news/meta-zuckerberg-ai-cloud-business-makes-sense Thu, 09 Jul 2026 18:35:33 +0000 TheNextWeb=5eec2f95a3bd04e1841a49493c787540 Mark Zuckerberg has publicly confirmed that Meta is exploring an AI cloud business. Selling access to computing power “makes sense”, he said, according to Bloomberg. The confirmation puts the CEO’s name to a plan first reported earlier this month. Meta had been said to be weighing a venture, dubbed Meta Compute, to rent out its spare AI […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> AI companies are spending hundreds of millions on the midterms, and they want one thing https://thenextweb.com/news/ai-companies-super-pac-election-spending-midterms Thu, 09 Jul 2026 18:27:17 +0000 TheNextWeb=0a2d9f3f51b8e4f9b3792154136ef78e AI companies and their backers are pouring vast sums into the 2026 US midterms through super PACs. The spending runs into the hundreds of millions across the sector, CNBC reports, and their central demand is remarkably consistent. They want a single national framework for AI, not a patchwork of state laws. The industry argues that 50 […] This story continues at The Next Web]]>
